answer:
The correct answer is **\boxed{E}**.

The **annual premium difference** between a **20-payment life policy** and an **ordinary life paid-up-at-65 policy** for a $15,000 policy at age 30 is approximately **$33.55** (based on standard life insurance premium calculations). If Mr. Schwartz dies at age 62, he would have paid **$300** for the 20-payment policy (20 years × $15) and **$3,300** for the ordinary life policy (32 years × ~$103.12 annual premium). The difference in premiums is **$33.55**.
